methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Anello Photonics is a Series B-2 stage company in the Defense & Security sector that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries, though the broader Defense & Security sector contains 24 identifiable peers.

A peer-median approach is not applicable here. The company lacks a sufficiently dense cluster of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within its valuation tier—zero datapoints met the filtering criteria requiring a minimum of three comparables. The broader all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given heterogeneity in stage, technology, and capital intensity across Defense & Security. The public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operate in different segments and at different scales, limiting direct transferability.

Without a triangulation of quantifiable methods, a precise valuation range cannot be derived from the available data. Further analysis would require either (1) detailed financial metrics (revenue, burn rate, growth trajectory) from the private peer set to enable revenue or margin multiples, (2) comparable transaction data from recent Series B-2 rounds in adjacent optics or sensor subsectors, or (3) explicit data on Anello's revenue and unit economics to anchor a discounted cash flow or comparable-company multiple. The key uncertainty remains the absence of narrow-set comps and operational financials for the target.
